vitals.feed_zones: self-supported; supermarkets, gas stations for resupply
vitals.cutoff_time: flexible Audax format with ferry timing constraints

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 79. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 73.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Self-supported; some book hotels for rest (e.g., Tenerife). Final La Gomera has mandatory overnight for fast riders.

final_verdict.one_liner: Epic island-hopping Audax on volcanic roads with ferry chases and Teide summit.

youtube_data.search_query: GranGuanche Audax Road gran fondo cycling Lanzarote

videos.title: How to ride the GranGuanche Bikepacking Audax. Part 1: Lanzarote
videos.description:
The GranGuanche is a bikepacking event across the Canary Islands and in this series of videos I show you how to ride the route.
Part 1 covers the first island of the event; Lanzarote.

For full event details see https://granguanche.com

A big thank you to the brands that support me:
Mason Cycles - https://masoncycles.cc
Hunt Bike wheels - https://www.huntbikewheels.com
Albion Cycling - https://www.albioncycling.com
Tailfin Bikepacking equipment - https://www.tailfin.cc/?v=79cba1185463
Rene Herse tyres - https://www.renehersecycles.com


If you like my videos and would like to help me produce more, please consider buying me a 'virtual coffee' at the link below, thanks!

https://www.buymeacoffee.com/jibbett887

videos.title: GGAG2
videos.description:
The GranGuanche Audax is a self-supported bikepacking event across the Canary Islands. 700km, 16000mt elevation, 5 islands, 4 ferries. It’s not a race, it's a collective adventure and a challenge to finish the route.

An Audax is a group ride that challenges to cover a distance within an established time limit. During the GranGuanche Audax, the time limit is set on each island by the ferry leaving for the next island.

videos.curation_reason: Official event content showcasing the race atmosphere and participant experiences across all five islands.

videos.title: BIKEPACKING : THE CANARY ISLANDS | GRAVEL RACE | GRANGUANCHE GRAVEL 2024
videos.description:
The GranGuanche Audax Gravel is a self-supported, single-stage, fixed-route, endurance cycling event across the Canary Islands. It’s not a race, it’s a collective adventure and a personal challenge to finish the route. There’s no cut-off time, there are no prizes, no medals, no brevet cards or finisher diplomas. The Audax is focused on the experience rather than the final results.

It’s a demanding route for experienced riders as it includes serious climbs, high mountains, and remote areas. Experienced means well-trained, technically skilled, and wisely equipped. Exhaustion, sleep deprivation, and night rides add even more risk to a challenging ride. Participants are fully responsible for their safety and logistics.

The Audax follows an extended version of the GranGuache GRAVEL route from Lanzarote to El Hierro for a total of about 800km and 17.000 mt climb.

The route mainly goes by on gravel roads and secondary paved roads but includes rocky single tracks, sandy sections and about 1-hour hike-a-bike. It’s open to any kind of bike, we recommend gravel bikes with low gearing and min 40mm wide tyres.

The Audax pace is the challenge to connect all the scheduled ferries in a non-stop ride, it’s only achievable by ultra-cycling athletes, the entry fee will be fully refunded to every rider fast enough to make it.

------

Join this channel and watch upcoming videos before anybody else!
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC7Sn5BSyeebQdMJtQ4jVqsw/join

If you enjoy the channel and would like to show your support you can find me on: https://buymeacoffee.com/mraudax - Thank you!

NukeProof Digger Factory 40T Front / 42T Rear
Shimano GRX810 1x11 groupset
Profile Design Legacy II Aerobars with 60mm risers
Garmin 530 GPS Cycling Computer
Tailfin Top Tube Bag, Aeropack and Fork Cage

- The Sleep Setup -

Naturehike Mongar 2 Person Tent - https://amzn.to/3ColHBZ
Vango Microlite 300 Sleeping Bag - https://amzn.to/3VgKtwC
Sea to Summit Reactor Plus Compact Themolite Mummy Liner - https://amzn.to/3SUMUDk
Collapsible Non-stick pan - https://amzn.to/3rIVbyx
Sea to Summit Collapsible Bowl - https://amzn.to/3yy7Sjh
Camping Windshield - https://amzn.to/3Cq8F75

- Video / Audio Stuff -

DJI Action Camera Wind Slayer - https://amzn.to/49Fwqqq
HoverAir X1 - Self-flying camera - https://uk.thehover.com/
ProStandard Grill Mount (mouth mount)

Please subscribe for regular vlogs and cycling videos.

quotes.text: It's got to be one of the hardest things I've ever done in my life got a hike bike oil and Hike it down the other side as well technical descent off the other side kind of underestimated that how hard this was going to be.

For me it was like a beautiful ride all over I had like a few moments of Doubt but most of the time I enjoyed a lot and I was happy and one guy he was talking to me saying hey why is he happy all the time and I said because look around you you are the most beautiful area you have the chance to cycle as long as you want with a lot of crazy people so why wouldn't I be happy.

key_challenges.name: Pico de las Nieves
key_challenges.description: The highest point on the island, described as a challenging 45k climb from sea level. One rider called it 'one of the hardest things I've ever done in my life' and noted you need to hike bike sections and navigate technical descents.

terrain_notes.text: Road surfaces are not great quality. Riders recommend wider tires than what you'd typically use in the UK due to surface conditions.
Includes technical descents that require hiking the bike down, with rough stuff sections throughout the route.

gear_mentions.text: Recommend minimum 28mm tires due to poor road surfaces. One rider switched from 25s to 28s specifically for these conditions.
Recommend something light and comfortable for the climbing. Free Motion rental bikes range from entry level to top spec with di2 and power meters.

race_day_tips.text: Use Free Motion's website map showing approved cafes and bike stops that sell local produce - essential for finding nutrition stops in the heat.
Ferry crossings can be rough - one rider felt very sick on the journey between islands.

additional_quotes.text: You get used to all this climbing like it's every day there's nothing you can do other than just up up when I came here I slowed down because you know in the UK you're just gonna fly but here you know you just take it a bit easier but yeah you sort of build your endurance

We've got the map on our website where all the bike stops are we going to sell Maps paper maps if you want because they have like approved cafes and restaurants they are located so we make sure they sell local produce and small businesses because in this heat we need it

rider_intel.search_text: The GranGuanche Audax Road covers multiple Canary Islands with the major challenge being Pico de las Nieves, the highest point requiring a 45k climb from sea level. Riders describe it as one of the hardest climbs they've experienced, with technical sections requiring hike-a-bike and challenging descents. Road surfaces are poor quality, prompting recommendations for minimum 28mm tires versus typical UK setups. The heat necessitates careful nutrition planning using approved cafe stops mapped by local bike centers. Ferry crossings between islands can be rough and cause seasickness. The constant elevation changes mean riders must adapt their pacing compared to flatter terrain, building endurance through sustained climbing efforts.
